---
title: "Pipeline"
---

One shared pipeline runs across all four sources, rather than a separate process per source. Each source differs only in its retrieval step; everything after that is identical.

## Retrieval

Each source is queried in its own way, since each has a different access mechanism:

- **OpenAlex**: API query filtered by Canadian institution and metascience-specific topic ID, as described in [Data Sources](data-sources.qmd). Where `has_fulltext` is true, the open-access PDF is retrieved.
- **Funding databases**: bulk data download where available, or the funder's own search interface, queried against the same bilingual term list.
- **Journals**: direct search of each venue for metascience-related content.
- **Research Data Canada / Digital Research Alliance**: their published working-group listings, fetched directly.

The output of this step, regardless of source, is a body of text: an acknowledgements section, a funding record's title and description, an article's content, a listing page's text.

## Extraction

Every piece of retrieved text is passed through the same fixed prompt, asking whether it names a real, ongoing initiative, distinct from a funder or institution name, and if so, to extract the name and any stated detail about it (people, affiliation, description).

For OpenAlex records specifically, full-text PDFs are first processed with GROBID to isolate the acknowledgements section, since that is where an initiative is most likely to be named in a publication that isn't directly about the initiative itself.

Each hit is logged as one row: candidate name, source, source URL or citation, date, extracted detail, and the raw source text retained for later verification.

## Deduplication

Because the same initiative can be named in more than one source, candidate names are compared against each other before confirmation: fuzzy string matching on the name itself, plus a check for shared people or institutions already extracted. Matches are merged into a single candidate record rather than confirmed twice under separate log entries.

## Confirmation

Each merged candidate is checked against one fixed rule: does it have its own documentation (a website, a repository, a page describing it), or is it independently named in at least two of the four sources. Candidates that pass are marked confirmed and move into the initiative record. Candidates that don't stay on the unconfirmed list, published alongside the dataset rather than discarded.

## Aggregation

For each confirmed initiative, the people named in connection with it seed the person layer, cross-checked against ORCID and OpenAlex author identifiers using the same disambiguation approach used in the author's other work reconstructing citation networks (BibVik). Publications connected to a confirmed initiative or its people are retrieved from OpenAlex and Crossref, supplemented by GROBID-extracted metadata where needed, and written into the publication layer.

## What this pipeline cannot guarantee

This process will not find every Canadian metascience initiative. It is most likely to miss initiatives with no online presence, informal groups that never documented themselves anywhere searchable, and work happening in languages or communities the source list and term list don't reach. The unconfirmed list and a dated register of known gaps are published specifically so this limitation stays visible and addressable over time, rather than implying the dataset is complete.
